race_name: RAGBRAI (Register's Annual Great Bicycle Ride Across Iowa)

top_categories.workout_context: 391 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Race-pace efforts that mimic RAGBRAI (Register's Annual Great Bicycle Ride Across Iowa)'s demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
391 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 391 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 16,027ft of climbing on paved Iowa roads — rolling hills, farm country, does to your power file on race day.

RAGBRAI (Register's Annual Great Bicycle Ride Across Iowa)'s paved Iowa roads — rolling hills, farm country, forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 391 miles.
16,027ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
RAGBRAI (Register's Annual Great Bicycle Ride Across Iowa)'s paved Iowa roads — rolling hills, farm country, rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.

Tempo is RAGBRAI (Register's Annual Great Bicycle Ride Across Iowa)'s race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 391 miles on paved Iowa roads — rolling hills, farm country,. Train it until it's automatic.
At RAGBRAI (Register's Annual Great Bicycle Ride Across Iowa), surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of RAGBRAI (Register's Annual Great Bicycle Ride Across Iowa). Train it until it feels boring.
16,027ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 391 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 391 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and HVLI Extended to prepare you for 391 miles of paved iowa roads — rolling hills, farm country, and river bluffs in Across Iowa, USA (Missouri River to Mississippi River, west to east).
